mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects accelerating real output and capacity utilization, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO surprises are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound constraint forces investors to reach for yield in equities, compressing risk premia and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns during FEDFUNDS < 0.25 episodes are negative on average or exhibit Sharpe ratio below the unconditional market Sharpe over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market that boosts consumer income and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with jointly rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE do not produce significantly positive 20-day SPX returns compared to periods with mixed signals (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recoveries, accelerating real GDP (GDPC1) growth predicts expanding corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; the same signal is nullified in late-cycle regimes when margins are already peaked.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises produce negative or statistically insignificant 60-day SPX returns during early-cycle quarters, or the signal's rank correlation with forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly jump in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following UNRATE spikes > 0.2pp are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with a stable or falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reflects a positive supply shock accommodated by loose monetary policy, preventing earnings-eroding tightening and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive INDPRO and flat/falling FEDFUNDS months fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns (mean return <= 0) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly proxies consumer spending momentum, the largest component of aggregate demand, and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days via earnings surprises in consumer-facing sectors.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day SPX forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 550 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and forward-looking weakness in construction, durable goods, and mortgage credit, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following HOUST < 550 are positive on average over a 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50%.
